steps.title: Apply the definition of conditional probability
steps.text: Use independence of X and Y to factor the joint probability:

steps.title: Substitute the Poisson PMFs
steps.text: Write each Poisson probability and use X + Y ~ Poisson(λ + μ) for the denominator:

steps.title: Cancel the exponential factors
steps.text: The terms e^{−λ}, e^{−μ}, and e^{−(λ+μ)} cancel completely, leaving only power terms:

steps.title: Identify as Binomial PMF
steps.text: Set p = λ/(λ+μ). The expression becomes the standard Binomial(n, p) probability mass function:

key_note: Conditioning a Poisson variable on the total of two independent Poissons always yields a Binomial distribution. The 'success probability' is the fraction of the total rate contributed by X.
exam_tip: For independent X ~ Poisson(λ) and Y ~ Poisson(μ), memorise: X | (X+Y=n) ~ Binomial(n, λ/(λ+μ)). This result appears in ISS Paper I, IIT JAM, and GATE Statistics almost every alternate year.
